sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Barcelona, sitting at the top of the league with 79 points, are set to host Mallorca, who are in 10th place with 44 points, in Round 33 at the Olímpic Lluís Companys in Barcelona. This matchup pits a dominant force against a mid-table side, with Barcelona looking to maintain their lead. Mallorca will aim to cause an upset in a challenging away fixture. The referee, Miguel Angel Ortiz Arias, averages 4.6 yellow cards per game and 0.33 red cards, indicating a potentially strict officiating style.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last encounter on December 3, 2024, Barcelona crushed Mallorca 5-1, with Raphinha earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.9 Sofascore rating. Over the last 5 matchups, Barcelona have dominated with 4 wins and 1 draw, leaving Mallorca without a victory. Historically, Barcelona have an unbeaten streak of 17 games against Mallorca, and they’ve been the first to score in 9 of the last 10 head-to-heads. Meanwhile, Mallorca have failed to keep a clean sheet in 20 consecutive games against Barcelona.

sections.subtitle: Barcelona’s Attacking Prowess
sections.text: Barcelona lead the league with 91 goals scored this season, ranking 1st in total goals and big chances created with 147. Their average ball possession of 68.88% is also the highest in the league, showcasing their control over games. Key players like Raphinha, with 16 goals (4th in the league) and 9 assists (2nd in the league), and an average rating of 7.85 (1st in the league), will be crucial. Lamine Yamal, with 12 assists (1st in the league) and 4.5 successful dribbles per game (1st in the league), is another standout. However, the absence of top scorer Robert Lewandowski, with 25 goals (1st in the league), could impact their finishing despite their depth.

sections.subtitle: Mallorca’s Struggles and Strengths
sections.text: Mallorca have had a less prolific season, with their goal distribution showing a late surge as 28.21% of their goals come between the 76th and 90th minutes. Their key player in form, Sergi Darder, has an average Sofascore rating of 7.52 over the last 5 matches, contributing 1 goal and 3 assists. Cyle Larin, with 6 goals, will lead their attack, but the absence of Vedat Muriqi, who has 7 goals, might hinder their chances. Defensively, Antonio Raíllo, with an average rating of 7.13, will need to be at his best to stop Barcelona’s relentless attack.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Barcelona, alongside Raphinha and Lamine Yamal, Pedri stands out with an average rating of 7.6 (4th in the league) and 62 key passes (4th in the league). Dani Olmo, with 9 goals and an average rating of 7.35, adds flair to the midfield. For Mallorca, apart from Sergi Darder, Johan Mojica provides width with 2 assists and an average rating of 6.8. Martin Valjent, a defender with 2 goals, could pose a threat from set pieces.

sections.subtitle: Team Streaks and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Barcelona have been the first to score in 7 of their last 8 matches, a trend that continues in 9 of their last 10 head-to-heads against Mallorca. They’ve also seen over 10.5 corners in 7 of their last 9 games, thanks to their league-leading average of 6.91 corners per game. Mallorca, on the other hand, have had under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 8 away games and under 10.5 corners in 6 of their last 6 matches. Both teams have seen under 4.5 cards in recent games, with Barcelona at 5 of 5 and Mallorca at 4 of 5, though head-to-heads have had over 4.5 cards in 5 of 7 recent encounters.

sections.subtitle: Barcelona’s Missing Firepower
sections.text: Barcelona will miss Robert Lewandowski, their leading scorer with 25 goals, which may affect their conversion rate despite ranking 1st in penalty area entries per game at 35.53. Other absentees include Alejandro Balde and Marc Casadó, potentially weakening their defensive and midfield balance. Mallorca are also without key players like Dani Rodríguez, with 7 assists, and Vedat Muriqi, which could limit their attacking options against a Barcelona side that ranks 4th in least goals conceded with 33.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Dominance and Timing
sections.text: Barcelona’s goal distribution shows they are dangerous late, scoring 21.21% of their goals between the 76th and 90th minutes, matching Mallorca’s vulnerability in the same period. Barcelona also rank 1st in possessions won in the final third per game at 5.41, indicating high pressing intensity. Mallorca’s defense will be tested, as Barcelona have hit the woodwork 25 times this season, the most in the league, signaling their bad luck in finishing despite creating 147 big chances.
